title: AB63 Program Records - Revenue Allocation, Policies, and Performance Data

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

SECTION A: PROGRAM-WIDE RECORDS

All records reflecting the City's internal policies and procedures for the AB63 discovery program, including how estimated gross receipts figures are generated when a taxpayer does not respond, and how cases are assigned, tracked, and resolved.

All records reflecting response time targets, service level agreements, or internal performance metrics for the AB63 Unit and the Office of Finance Customer Service Division.

All records reflecting the total number of AB63 notices issued in 2024 and 2025, broken down by month if available.

All records reflecting the rate at which AB63 assessments are disputed, corrected, or reduced following taxpayer response, for the years 2022 to the present.

All records reflecting complaints filed against the Office of Finance related to incorrect assessments, delayed responses, or interest accrual on disputed or unresolved amounts, for the years 2022 to the present.

All records reflecting communications between the Office of Finance and the California Franchise Tax Board regarding the AB63 program generally, including any data sharing agreements, program guidelines, or correspondence about program scope and implementation.

All records reflecting any audits, reviews, or evaluations of the AB63 program conducted internally or by any outside body, including any findings related to accuracy of assessments or taxpayer response handling.

All records reflecting the average time between the Office of Finance receiving FTB data identifying an unregistered business and the issuance of a discovery notice to that business, for the years 2015 to the present.

All records reflecting the Office of Finance's policies regarding the period for which interest may be assessed on unregistered businesses, specifically in cases where the City had FTB data identifying the business prior to issuing a notice.

All records reflecting any legal opinions, memoranda, or guidance regarding whether interest may be assessed for periods predating the issuance of a discovery notice.

All records reflecting communications between the Office of Finance and the City Attorney regarding the legality or appropriateness of assessing interest for periods during which a taxpayer had not yet been notified of their tax liability.

SECTION B: REVENUE ALLOCATION RECORDS

All records reflecting how revenue generated by the AB63 program has been allocated, distributed, or designated for affordable housing purposes, including any accounting records, budget allocations, fund transfers, grant disbursements, or reports on funds distributed, from 2001 to the present.

All records reflecting how revenue generated by the AB63 program has been allocated or spent on business tax reform, including any accounting records, budget allocations, policy changes funded by AB63 revenue, or reports on funds disbursed, from 2001 to the present.

All records reflecting the total revenue collected through the AB63 program annually, broken down by principal, interest, and penalties separately, from 2001 to the present.

All records reflecting any legal definition, internal policy, or guidelines used by the Office of Finance to define "affordable housing" and "business tax reform" for purposes of AB63 revenue allocation.

I request that records be provided in electronic format where possible. If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify each record withheld, the specific exemption claimed, and the factual basis for the exemption as required by 
Government Code Section 7922.000.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ben Loeffler

title: NOTICE OF STATUTORY REQUEST – CPRA (Gov. Code § 7920) & IPA (Civ. Code § 1798) (University of California, Berkeley)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

To whom it may concern,

I, Young Jin Jung (SID: 3036184384), formally request the following records pursuant to the California Public Records Act (CPRA) and the Information Practices Act of 1977 (IPA). Please note that under Cal. Const. Art. I, § 3(b), any statute or rule that limits the right of access must be "narrowly construed."

1. Identification of the Information Source & Submitter (Case No. 019240-001-2024):

Reporting Party Identification: All records identifying the specific individual, office, or third party that first provided the "information alleging" my involvement in the behavior described in Case No. 019240-001-2024. I request the identity of the person who reported the alleged forgery to the Center for Student Conduct (CSC).

Verification Correspondence: All records of communication between the College of L&S and the Philosophy 132 instructor regarding the verification of the signature, including timestamps and the specific content of those communications.

All metadata and audit trails (IP addresses, timestamps, and portal logs). Access is requested under Gov. Code § 7922.570, which requires the disclosure of electronic records in their native format.

2-a. Comprehensive Internal Records & Legal Directives (2022 – 2026):

All emails, Slack/Teams logs, internal case notes (SIS/CalCentral), and summaries of verbal communications involving: Office of Legal Counsel (UC Berkeley Legal Team), Psychology Dept, CSI (Center for Support and Intervention), UCPD/Police, BIO, SAO, DSP, UHS/Social Services, and other school staff (including Roseanne Fong, Tim Cahill, Zack, Sheri L. Johnson, Marilyn de la Cruz, Jan Engelmann, and Kate O'Neill).

This request includes any legal assessments, directives, or risk management instructions provided by the Legal Counsel to the aforementioned departments regarding my student file.

Legal Pre-emption of Redaction: As the data subject, any "Privacy" exemptions under Gov. Code § 7927.700 or FERPA are inapplicable to me. Under Civ. Code § 1798.34, I have an absolute right to inspect all personal 
information in my file without redaction.

2-b Dispatch & Incident Records (2312 Warring St, Berkeley, CA 94704):

All dispatch logs, call records, and incident reports regarding any responses to 2312 Warring St in 2022 and 2023 involving me.

Identification of Personnel: Specific records identifying the agency and affiliation of all responding officers. Please clarify whether these individuals were sworn UCPD officers, private security contractors, or Federal officers/agents.

Any contracts or mutual aid agreements that authorized non-UCPD personnel to operate at this location during the specified period.

3. Committee & Third-Party Records:

All deliberation records and logs of any University Staff (2023-2026) and any communications with/regarding Rakesh Bhandari.

The "Deliberative Process Privilege" does not apply to purely factual materials or logs that form the basis of administrative actions.

4. Financial Disclosures (Sheri L. Johnson):

All records of external funding, grants, and compensation (2023-2025). This is a matter of significant public interest involving the financial integrity of public officials.

Formal Notice: If any portion of this request is denied, you must provide a written response within 10 days (Gov. Code § 7922.535) and "demonstrate that the record in question is exempt" under Gov. Code § 7922.000.

NOTICE OF POTENTIAL LITIGATION:
Please be advised that any further failure to comply with this request will result in a Petition for Writ of Mandate filed in the Superior Court. This petition will not only address current non-compliance but will also incorporate all previous instances of PRA violations that were heretofore tolerated. Your persistent refusal to disclose public records constitutes a "pattern of practice" that violates both the letter and the spirit of the law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Young Jin Jung

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request – Missing Mail, Delivery Irregularities, and Complaint Handling (Michael T. Pace – PO Box 1033 / 37 Corey Rd, Hancock MA – November 2025 to Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552), this request seeks records related to missing mail, delivery irregularities, and the handling of prior complaints associated with United States Postal Service operations in Lanesborough, Massachusetts, including mail directed to PO Box 1033 and/or addresses associated with Jiminy Peak Mountain Resort.

This request relates to mail addressed to Michael T. Pace and associated delivery locations, including PO Box 1033 (Lanesborough, MA) and 37 Corey Road, Hancock, Massachusetts 01237-1033.

This request arises from repeated instances in which mail expected to be delivered or forwarded was not received, despite the existence of an active USPS PO Box (PO Box 1033) designated as the correct delivery address. Prior communications with USPS indicate that certain physical addresses associated with the property were not valid for delivery or forwarding, raising questions as to how mail addressed to those locations was handled.

This request includes, but is not limited to:

• Any complaint logs, service requests, or case records submitted regarding missing, undelivered, improperly forwarded, or held mail  
• Any records reflecting internal USPS review, investigation, escalation, or closure of such complaints  
• Any internal or external communications (including emails, memoranda, or notes) referencing these issues, the relevant addresses, or associated delivery points  
• Any delivery records, scans, carrier notes, or routing data sufficient to determine whether mail was delivered, misdirected, held, returned, or otherwise not received  
• Any records reflecting known delivery issues, address flags, business classifications, or restrictions affecting delivery or forwarding at the relevant location(s)  
• Any records reflecting coordination between USPS personnel, local post offices, or regional offices regarding handling of mail for the relevant address(es)  
• Any records indicating whether complaints were referred to supervisory personnel, postal inspectors, or oversight entities  

The timeframe for this request is November 1, 2025 through the present.

If any responsive records are withheld, please provide the specific statutory basis for each denial.

I request that all responsive records be provided in electronic format where available.

I also request a waiver of any fees, as this request concerns the reliability and integrity of mail delivery and is made in the public interest.

If this request is more appropriately directed to another office, please forward it accordingly.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Thomas Pace

title: Public Records Request – Escalated Fire and Life Safety Complaint (Obstructed Means of Egress – Jiminy Peak, Hancock, MA)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request – Escalated Fire and Life Safety Complaint (Obstructed Means of Egress – Jiminy Peak, Hancock, MA)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law (M.G.L. c. 66, § 10), I hereby request copies of all records related to a fire and life safety complaint concerning obstructed means of egress at Jiminy Peak Mountain Resort, located in Hancock, Massachusetts.

On or about January 31, 2026, a complaint was submitted regarding items placed within a designated exit path in the interior hallway outside Unit 844. The condition includes a trash receptacle and other objects reducing the clear width of egress, despite posted signage indicating that such items are not permitted in the hallway.

This situation appears to implicate applicable provisions of 527 CMR, NFPA 101, and the Massachusetts State Building Code requiring that means of egress remain clear and unobstructed at all times.

This matter has been raised with local and state-level entities, including the Town of Hancock and the Massachusetts Department of Fire Services, and has also been submitted through public records channels. No confirmation has been received that the condition has been reviewed, inspected, or corrected.

This request includes, but is not limited to:

• Any records received from or shared with the Town of Hancock, Hancock Fire Department, or local code enforcement authorities regarding this complaint  
• Any communications, referrals, or escalations involving the Massachusetts Department of Fire Services or Department of Public Safety and Inspections  
• Any internal or external communications (including emails, memoranda, or notes) referencing this incident or property  
• Any inspection records, reports, or determinations related to Jiminy Peak Mountain Resort concerning means of egress  
• Any records indicating whether this matter has been reviewed, investigated, or remains open  

The timeframe for this request is January 1, 2026 through the present.

If any responsive records are withheld, please provide the specific statutory basis for each denial.

I request that all responsive records be provided in electronic format where available.

I also request a waiver of any fees, as this request concerns fire and life safety conditions affecting building occupants and is made in the public interest.

If this request is more appropriately directed to another office, please forward it accordingly.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Thomas Pace

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All lists, records, notes, inventory forms, of all property seized from Bonnie Silveria, Elgio Limeta, or Sean Derning on Binford Road, between January 1st, 2023-March 2026.

All pictures associated with all lists, records, inventory forms, of property seized on Binford Road between from Bonnie Silveria, Elgio Limeta, or Sean Derning January 1st, 2023-March 2026.

All inventory forms, pictures, records, made by Julia Barnes or her subordinates, pertaining to property seized from Bonnie Silveria, Elgio Limeta, or Sean Derningon Binford Road between January 1st, 2023-March 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robbie Powelson

title: LIHTC & MTBA Documents

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: LIHTC & MTBA Documents
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

(1) A log or table of the LIHTC 9% competitive submissions/applications for 2018 (equivalent to the posting that exists for other years like 2020 at https://s3.amazonaws.com/thda.org/Documents/Business-Partners/Multi-Family-Developers/LIHTC-Program/2020-Competitive-Submissions-for-Website.pdf).
(2) The same kind log or table for the 2019 submissions. 
(3) A log or table of the MTBA firm applications received for round 2 of 2024 (equivalent to the posting, for round 1 of 2024, at https://dogvxws799i6n.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/MTBA-Round-1-Applications.pdf).
(4) A log or table of the MTBA firm applications received for round 1 of 2023.
(5) A log or table of the MTBA firm applications received for round 2 of 2023.
(6) A log or table of the MTBA Closings in 2025 (equivalent to the posting, for the year 2024, at https://dogvxws799i6n.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/2024-MTBA-Listing-for-Website-Closing_Carryforward-1.pdf).

Note that the first five of these documents pertain to applications submitted, not awards allocated. For context, also note that this request was preceded by an attempt to locate these documents on the THDA website. Files were successfully located for other years, but not for those requested above. For #4, a document was found, but the version on the website does not include a THDA # (project ID), which is unusual. The documents sought in this request are expected to include this field, if it at all exists.

In case any of the six logs of applications/closings only appear as part of a larger spreadsheet or database extract that covers several years or rounds, such a record would also be considered valid toward satisfying this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Lars Brown.

title: CIA: MDR: CIA Weekly Summary Special Report, "Moscow and the Georgians," dated 15 December 1972 (Document No. CIA-RDP85T00875R001500040043-1)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: CIA: MDR: CIA Weekly Summary Special Report, "Moscow and the Georgians," dated 15 December 1972 (Document No. CIA-RDP85T00875R001500040043-1)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I. Basis for Request
Pursuant to Section 3.5 of Executive Order 13526, I hereby submit this Mandatory Declassification Review (MDR) request concerning the document identified below. Section 3.5(a) of Executive Order 13526 provides that individuals may request a review of classified information for declassification, and that agencies shall conduct a review of such information to determine whether it continues to meet the standards for classification under the Order.

II. Document Identification
Document Title: Weekly Summary Special Report — "Moscow and the Georgians"
Originating Agency: Central Intelligence Agency, Directorate of Intelligence
Document Date: 15 December 1972
Document Number/CREST ID: CIA-RDP85T00875R001500040043-1
Declassification Review Date: Approved for Release 2005/01/11
Publicly Available At: https://www.cia.gov/readingroom/docs/CIA-RDP85T00875R001500040043-1.pdf

III. Description of the Document and Nature of This Request
The document at issue is a five-page CIA Directorate of Intelligence Weekly Summary Special Report, classified SECRET at the time of composition, concerning Soviet domestic politics — specifically Moscow's relationship with the Georgian Soviet Socialist Republic and the political downfall of Georgian Communist Party First Secretary Vasily Mzhavanadze following the exposure of widespread economic corruption. The report was approved for partial release in January 2005 but contains multiple redactions citing Exemption 25X1 — the statutory exemption protecting intelligence sources and methods under 50 U.S.C. § 3024(i), as codified through the automatic declassification framework of E.O. 13526. This MDR request challenges the continued application of those 25X1 exemptions on the grounds that the information withheld no longer meets the standards for continued classification under Section 1.1 and Section 3.3 of Executive Order 13526. Specifically, the requestor asserts that:

All named individuals referenced in or near the redacted passages are deceased, eliminating any personal privacy or source-protection rationale for continued withholding;
The underlying historical events — Soviet-era Georgian party politics and corruption investigations of the early 1970s — are thoroughly documented in open academic, journalistic, and governmental literature;
The Soviet state itself ceased to exist on December 25, 1991 — more than three decades ago — eliminating any foreign government sensitivity; and
The passage of over fifty years since the document's creation triggers the presumption of declassification under Section 3.3(a) of E.O. 13526.

The requestor specifically requests a line-by-line review of each redacted passage in the released version of the document.

IV. Deceased Status of All Named Individuals
A core rationale for applying 25X1 exemptions to documents concerning named foreign nationals is the protection of sources who might be identified through context, and who might face retaliation. That rationale is entirely inapplicable here. Every named individual in or proximate to the redacted passages in this document is deceased:

Vasily Mzhavanadze — Georgian SSR First Secretary; died 1988.
Eduard Shevardnadze — Mzhavanadze's successor; later Soviet Foreign Minister and President of Georgia; died July 7, 2014.
Nikolay Tskhakaya — Tbilisi city party secretary; removed from post in May 1972; deceased.
Otari Lolashvili — Georgian MVD chief referenced in the document; deceased.
Leonid Brezhnev — General Secretary of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union; died November 10, 1982.
Pyotr Shelest — Ukrainian party boss referenced in the document; died January 22, 1996.
Heydar Aliyev — Azerbaijan KGB chief referenced by analogy; died December 12, 2003.
The death of all persons identified in or near redacted passages eliminates any source-protection, retaliation-risk, or personal-privacy rationale for continued withholding under 25X1.

Thank you to the agency and for their commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Fog Data Science Contracts, Polices, Use Logs, and Marketing

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Fog Data Science Contracts, Polices, Use Logs, and Marketing
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

The Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) is submitting this request for records under the Florida Sunshine Law. 

The requested records are those describing and detailing the relationship and any agreements between this agency and Fog Data Science (www.fogdatascience.com) for the period from January 1, 2020 through the date on which this request is processed.

Responsive records include, but are not limited to, documents concerning or mentioning Fog Data Science, including all documentation regarding Fog Reveal and any other service or software provided by or created by Fog Data Science, LLC.

CONTRACT AND AGREEMENTS
• All contracts, memorandums of understanding, and any other written agreements between this agency and Fog Data Science, including all associated amendments, exhibits, and supplemental and supporting documentation.
• All bidding materials provided by Fog Data Science (or any subsidiary thereof), as well as all competing bids and a copy of the relevant Request for Proposals 

GUIDING DOCUMENTATION and POLICIES
• All policies, memorandums of understanding, and other guidance materials generated by this agency and/or any entity operating on its behalf or to its benefit (including, but not limited to, any unions, community groups, other government agencies, or individuals) regarding the acquisition and use of any data or services provided by Fog Data Science or any subdivision thereof. 
• All user manuals, training materials, guidance documents, or related materials obtained from Fog Data Science or the Fog Reveal platform (hosted at www.fogreveal.com and www.fogds.com). According to Fog Data Science’s portal: “Users agree by accessing this portal that they are governed by their agency/sponsor’s policies and guidelines in the use of the portal and any data and analysis resulting from its use.” Records responsive to this section would include those applicable policies and guidelines. 

DOCUMENTATION OF USE
• Any query logs capturing this agency’s use of Fog Data Science tools and the Fog Reveal platform. Should query logs exist for any other platform, service, or software created and/or provided by Fog Data Science, these materials are also responsive to this request.
• Any audits and reports of audits of use of Fog Data Science and the Fog Reveal platform.
• Any data-sharing agreements, terms of use, licensing agreements, and any other agreements relevant to the use of Fog Data Science services or data collected through use of these systems.

MARKETING and PROMOTION MATERIALS
• All promotional, descriptive, and marketing materials in the possession of this agency related to and/or referencing “Fog Data Science” and/or “Fog Reveal.” This includes all presentations, marketing materials, technical documentation, proposals, or similar records shared with this agency and/or any representative thereof by Fog Data Science and/or any representative thereof.

We ask that these materials be provided on a rolling basis; we would appreciate it if your office would provide those materials to us as your office collects and reviews responsive records. Please contact us for further discussion regarding the search and release of these materials.

This request is being made as part of research in the public interest. It is not being made for commercial purposes. 

As this request is being made in the public interest and not for commercial purposes, please assess fees appropriately. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform us of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. 

I request that all records be disclosed in their native, digital format. 

Please transmit responsive materials electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.

If you need any more information in order to process the request, or cannot process a portion of the request for any reason, please respond to the email address from which you received this request. Further discussion may be conducted via email. Please let us know if a phone call will be necessary or desirable; such contact information will be provided upon request.


Thank you very much for your time and help. I’ll look forward to hearing from your office again.


Sincerely,

Beryl Lipton

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P418525-033126
communications.communication:
Dear Beryl  Lipton: 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Miami-Dade Sheriff's Office.  Your request has been received and is being processed.  Your request was received in this office on 3/31/2026 and given the reference number P418525-033126 for tracking purposes.  To Whom It May Concern: 

The Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) is submitting this request for records under the Florida Sunshine Law. 

The requested records are those describing and detailing the relationship and any agreements between this agency and Fog Data Science (www.fogdatascience.com) for the period from January 1, 2020 through the date on which this request is processed. 

Responsive records include, but are not limited to, documents concerning or mentioning Fog Data Science, including all documentation regarding Fog Reveal and any other service or software provided by or created by Fog Data Science, LLC. 

CONTRACT AND AGREEMENTS 
• All contracts, memorandums of understanding, and any other written agreements between this agency and Fog Data Science, including all associated amendments, exhibits, and supplemental and supporting documentation. 
• All bidding materials provided by Fog Data Science (or any subsidiary thereof), as well as all competing bids and a copy of the relevant Request for Proposals 

GUIDING DOCUMENTATION and POLICIES 
• All policies, memorandums of understanding, and other guidance materials generated by this agency and/or any entity operating on its behalf or to its benefit (including, but not limited to, any unions, community groups, other government agencies, or individuals) regarding the acquisition and use of any data or services provided by Fog Data Science or any subdivision thereof. 
• All user manuals, training materials, guidance documents, or related materials obtained from Fog Data Science or the Fog Reveal platform (hosted at www.fogreveal.com and www.fogds.com). According to Fog Data Science’s portal: “Users agree by accessing this portal that they are governed by their agency/sponsor’s policies and guidelines in the use of the portal and any data and analysis resulting from its use.” Records responsive to this section would include those applicable policies and guidelines. 

DOCUMENTATION OF USE 
• Any query logs capturing this agency’s use of Fog Data Science tools and the Fog Reveal platform. Should query logs exist for any other platform, service, or software created and/or provided by Fog Data Science, these materials are also responsive to this request. 
• Any audits and reports of audits of use of Fog Data Science and the Fog Reveal platform. 
• Any data-sharing agreements, terms of use, licensing agreements, and any other agreements relevant to the use of Fog Data Science services or data collected through use of these systems. 

MARKETING and PROMOTION MATERIALS 
• All promotional, descriptive, and marketing materials in the possession of this agency related to and/or referencing “Fog Data Science” and/or “Fog Reveal.” This includes all presentations, marketing materials, technical documentation, proposals, or similar records shared with this agency and/or any representative thereof by Fog Data Science and/or any representative thereof. 

We ask that these materials be provided on a rolling basis; we would appreciate it if your office would provide those materials to us as your office collects and reviews responsive records. Please contact us for further discussion regarding the search and release of these materials. 

This request is being made as part of research in the public interest. It is not being made for commercial purposes. 

As this request is being made in the public interest and not for commercial purposes, please assess fees appropriately. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform us of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. 

I request that all records be disclosed in their native, digital format. 

Please transmit responsive materials electronically, by e-mail attachment if available. 

If you need any more information in order to process the request, or cannot process a portion of the request for any reason, please respond to the email address from which you received this request. Further discussion may be conducted via email. Please let us know if a phone call will be necessary or desirable; such contact information will be provided upon request. 

Thank you very much for your time and help. I’ll look forward to hearing from your office again. 

Sincerely, 

Beryl Lipton 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2253120/ 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant person(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request.  You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. NOTE: If there is no action taken on a request by a requestor, the request will be considered abandoned and withdrawn after 30 days and will require the submission of a new request. 
To monitor the progress or update this request please log into the Public Records Center. title: Records Request (Henry County Public Schools)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of all school bus stop-arm camera contracts and amendments which includes the following:

-The total number of buses in their fleet;
-The total number of buses serving special needs students;
-The total number of buses installed with a stop arm camera system; and
-The total number of buses serving special needs students installed with a stop arm camera system.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to you acknowledgement.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith
